commentaries.author: Ephrem The Syrian
commentaries.content: “The sun will go down” upon those prophets who lead my people astray, dwelling in darkness. “It will be night for you, without vision, and it will be dark for you, without dawn.” “For, when [the spirit] goes out of a man, it goes about wandering.” It does this quite by nature. [The Lord] rendered this judgment with regard to [the people]: “Thus will it be for this generation.” That is, in the days of the prophets the evil spirit had gone out from them, [that spirit] that was sin itself. Commentary of Tatian’s Diatessaron

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Peace. They pretend goodness, while they do the greatest mischief.
Prepare. Literally, "sanctify "(Haydock) or denounce war. (Calmet)
False prophets seek their private lucre. (Worthington)

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
